name: 5-hydroxytryptophan (5-HTP)
mechanism: Immediate serotonin precursor formed from tryptophan and decarboxylated by aromatic L-amino-acid decarboxylase to serotonin; strongest mechanism is precursor loading rather than receptor binding.
contraindications: SSRIs/SNRIs/MAOIs/triptans and other serotonergic agents (additive ↑ serotonin-toxicity risk)
carbidopa (can increase 5-HTP exposure and GI/serotonergic adverse effects).
summary: Immediate serotonin precursor formed from tryptophan and decarboxylated by aromatic L-amino-acid decarboxylase to serotonin; strongest mechanism is precursor loading rather than receptor binding.
